assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements.
The foreign exchange exposure relates to an intragroup dividend receivable that has already been recognised.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for a recognised asset or liability exposed to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of a recognised asset or liability or a highly probable forecast transaction.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
